g.data-narration: Ideation in UI UX Design. The creative process of generating a broad set of solutions and ideas.
g.text: Generating creative solutions through structured techniques

g.data-narration: The Design Thinking process has five stages: Empathize, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test. Ideation is the third and most creative stage where ideas flow freely.

Key brainstorming techniques include Mind Mapping with a central idea branching outward, Sketching ideas quickly, How Might We questions to reframe challenges, and Crazy Eights for rapid idea generation in eight minutes.

Popular ideation methods include SCAMPER which uses prompts like Substitute, Combine, Adapt, and Modify. Worst Possible Idea inverts thinking. Brainwriting allows silent written idea generation. And Storyboarding maps out user journeys visually.

Ideation uses both divergent and convergent thinking. In the divergent phase, you generate as many ideas as possible expanding outward. In the convergent phase, you filter and select the best ideas, narrowing down to actionable solutions.

Remember four key principles of effective ideation. Quantity over quality, generate as many ideas as possible. Defer judgment, no criticism during ideation. Build on others ideas to create even better solutions. And go wild, the craziest ideas often lead to breakthrough innovations.
